mappings.rationaleAI: The 1937-1945 Sino-Japanese War merged into the broader Pacific theatre after Pearl Harbor, directly constituting the rise and fall of Japanese military power (A.2) that engulfed Oceania and threatened Australia, reshaping the region's strategic landscape and affecting indigenous populations.

British imperial governance across the Pacific and Australasia underpins the post-WWI mandate system in Oceania (A.1), post-WWII decolonization and autonomy movements among dependent territories (A.3), and Australia's transition from dominion status to independent foreign policy including granting Papua New Guinea independence in 1975 (B.3).
